title: Add Trust documentation and demo

sources.reason: Defines the Trust documentation and demo acceptance boundary.
Defines the authoritative evidence flow and component boundaries.
Defines the governed task lifecycle and stop conditions.
Defines machine-readable readiness and fail-closed review signals.

riskAssessment.reason: Documentation and a local deterministic demo have low execution risk; inaccurate claims are the main review risk.

executionDecision.reason: Documentation scope, evidence sources, acceptance, and verification are explicit.

preReviewWarnings: Review that the demo reports blocked scenarios as evidence and does not imply that narrative explanations authorize execution.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: README and authoritative documentation explain the Trust Layer boundary, evidence model, human decision protocol, safe recovery, and non-goals.
The deterministic demo shows at least three failure demonstrations, blocks unsafe operations, and reports zero unsafe operations in machine-readable output.
Examples explicitly distinguish repository evidence from agent explanations and self-declared approval.
Documentation metadata and project checks pass, and the demo exits successfully without network or production access.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: The Trust Layer is implemented but its boundary, fail-closed behavior, and evidence model are difficult to discover from the entry documentation.
constraints: Keep documentation claims aligned with executable repository behavior.
Do not present agent explanations, screenshots, or self-declared approvals as independent evidence.
Preserve the existing documentation architecture and bilingual entry points.
The demo must be deterministic, local-only, and perform zero unsafe operations.
intent.rationale: A concise Trust Layer guide and failure-oriented demo make the new controls reviewable and teach adopters when the system must stop.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: Set only when a human explicitly approves restricted governance paths.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
